summary: Crystal‑clear audience tape of a fire‑filled show highlighted by an immense Deal and a near‑perfect Wharf Rat
review: The Walker‑Scotton‑Miller audience recording is exceptionally clear, well balanced and almost sounds like a soundboard, delivering every instrument with crisp definition. Musically the concert shines – thundering Jack Straw, soaring Peggy‑O, an endlessly repeated Deal chorus and a centerpiece Wharf Rat that builds into one of the band’s most intense jams, despite a couple of stray notes from Jerry.

ai_rating.rationale: All but one reviewer gave 5 stars and praised both performance and audio; the single 4‑star note only mildly tempers an otherwise flawless consensus.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Highly praised for being clear, crisp and well balanced with minimal crowd noise; several reviewers say it sounds like a soundboard or matrix after digital mastering by Charlie Miller.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Standard two‑set layout that builds nicely; first set packs the classic faves, second set crescendos with an epic Wharf Rat jam and extended drum passages.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as "on fire" with a perfect tone on many songs; crushing Wharf Rat solo though he slips a couple of bad notes toward the end.
band_member_comments.Drums: Long, cool drum segments highlighted as a strong point of the show.
